perturbation.original_text: For the purpose of the present Agreement, “Mater ial” is defined as any data, test, study or any information in support of the Registration under REACH made available to the Consortium or generated or determined or used in any way by the Consortium to comply with the requirements of REACH and provided to the Re ceiving Party for the purpose of registration under REACH.
perturbation.changed_text: For the purpose of the present Agreement, “Mater ial” is defined as any data, test, study or any information in support of the Registration under REACH made available to the Consortium or generated or determined or used in any way by the Consortium to comply with the requirements of REACH and provided to the Re ceiving Party. For purposes of clarity in Section 3, 'Material' refers *only* to published documents, not to any underlying data.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Material' is restricted in section 1 to exclude underlying data, while Section 3 speaks about data, creating a contradiction regarding the scope of permitted disclosure. The change introduces a specific caveat that directly conflicts with the broader original definition when the 'Material' is defined in Section 3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Permitted Disclosure of Material a. The Receiving Party shall solely disclose th e Material to its affiliates, employees and persons in the Receiving Party’s organizati on who (i) have responsibility for or are directly concerned with the purposes of this Agreement and of the Consortium Agreement; (ii) are subject to non-disclosu re or confidentiality obligations to the Receiving Party (the Material constituting protected information for purposes of such obligations); and (iii) have been informed and are fully aware of the Receiving Party’s obligations under this agreement and under the Consortium Agreement. b. The Receiving Party may provide its customers with (i) Safety Data Sheets as defined in Article 31 of REACH, (ii) re levant exposure scenarios, or (iii) other available and relevant information about the substance that is necessary to enable appropriate risk management measures to be identified and applied. c. The Receiving Party may disclose th e Material if and to the exte nt that such disclosure is required by law or court order, provided that the Receiving Party notifies the Disclosing Page 2 of 5 LND99 448148-1.049002.0010 Molybdenum Consortium, First Amendment Jan 2008 Party. Any disclosure of Data and/or Studies that could result in public disclosure of the Data and/or Studies shall on ly be permissible after prio r approval from the Steering Committee or the IMOA Executive Committee. d. The Receiving Party and its Affiliates may use the Material for compliance with laws and regulations in other non-EU jurisdictions provid ed that the confidentia lity of the Material is guaranteed and in compliance with the NDA. Any disclosure of the Data and/or Studies for the purposes of compliance with non-EU regulat ory requirements that could result in public disclosure of the Data and/or Studies shall only be permissible after prior approval from the Steering Committee or the IMOA Executive Committee. e. For the avoidance of doubt, nothing in this Non-Disclosure Agreem ent shall prevent an Individual Member from supplyi ng Data and/or Studies in it s possession or control to a Governmental Authority where required to do so by law or regulation and where refusing to do so would require the Individual Memb er to violate that law or regulation and subject the Individual Member to fines or other sanction by the Governmental Authority.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Material' broadly to include any data, test, study, or information related to REACH registration. The changed text narrows this definition by stating that, specifically in Section 3, 'Material' refers *only* to published documents and *not* to any underlying data. The contradicted text (Section 3) discusses the 'Permitted Disclosure of Material' which now has two contradictory definitions depending on which part of the document one is reading, especially given the specific carveout against including underlying data. This new caveat directly contradicts the rest of the document which speaks about 'Data and/or Studies'. This is a strong contradiction because it impacts the scope of permitted disclosure, leading to uncertainty regarding what is considered 'Material' and subject to the disclosure terms in Section 3.

perturbation.original_text: Unless defined herein, the definitions in this Agreemen t shall be the same as those set forth in the Consortium Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless defined herein, the definitions in this Agreemen t shall be the same as those set forth in the Consortium Agreement, *except for 'Receiving Party' which, for the purpose of this NDA, shall mean only the direct signatory and not its affiliates*.
perturbation.explanation: This change alters the definition of 'Receiving Party' compared to the Consortium Agreement, creating a contradiction. Section 3 refers to affiliates. By explicitly stating that the definition of “Receiving Party” excludes its affiliates for this NDA, there is a direct conflict in the scope of who is bound to protect and disclose the materials under Section 3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Permitted Disclosure of Material a. The Receiving Party shall solely disclose th e Material to its affiliates, employees and persons in the Receiving Party’s organizati on who (i) have responsibility for or are directly concerned with the purposes of this Agreement and of the Consortium Agreement; (ii) are subject to non-disclosu re or confidentiality obligations to the Receiving Party (the Material constituting protected information for purposes of such obligations); and (iii) have been informed and are fully aware of the Receiving Party’s obligations under this agreement and under the Consortium Agreement. b. The Receiving Party may provide its customers with (i) Safety Data Sheets as defined in Article 31 of REACH, (ii) re levant exposure scenarios, or (iii) other available and relevant information about the substance that is necessary to enable appropriate risk management measures to be identified and applied. c. The Receiving Party may disclose th e Material if and to the exte nt that such disclosure is required by law or court order, provided that the Receiving Party notifies the Disclosing Page 2 of 5 LND99 448148-1.049002.0010 Molybdenum Consortium, First Amendment Jan 2008 Party. Any disclosure of Data and/or Studies that could result in public disclosure of the Data and/or Studies shall on ly be permissible after prio r approval from the Steering Committee or the IMOA Executive Committee. d. The Receiving Party and its Affiliates may use the Material for compliance with laws and regulations in other non-EU jurisdictions provid ed that the confidentia lity of the Material is guaranteed and in compliance with the NDA. Any disclosure of the Data and/or Studies for the purposes of compliance with non-EU regulat ory requirements that could result in public disclosure of the Data and/or Studies shall only be permissible after prior approval from the Steering Committee or the IMOA Executive Committee. e. For the avoidance of doubt, nothing in this Non-Disclosure Agreem ent shall prevent an Individual Member from supplyi ng Data and/or Studies in it s possession or control to a Governmental Authority where required to do so by law or regulation and where refusing to do so would require the Individual Memb er to violate that law or regulation and subject the Individual Member to fines or other sanction by the Governmental Authority.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the definitions in the agreement are the same as those in the Consortium Agreement unless defined within the agreement. The changed text explicitly excludes affiliates from the definition of 'Receiving Party' for the purpose of the NDA, which directly contradicts Section 3, which allows the Receiving Party to disclose material to its affiliates. This creates a strong in-text contradiction as it alters the scope of who is bound by the confidentiality obligations in Section 3, therefore making it an instance of "Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction".

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall take all reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or improper use of the Material, to prevent the material from falling into the public domain, and to protect it from falling into the possession of Third Parties. Such measures include, but shall not be limited to, the highest de gree of care that the Receiving Party utilises to protect its own confidential information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall take commercially reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or improper use of the Material, to prevent the material from falling into the public domain, and to protect it from falling into the possession of Third Parties. Such measures include, but shall not be limited to, the usual degree of care that the Receiving Party utilises to protect its own confidential information.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'all reasonable measures' with 'commercially reasonable measures' and 'highest degree' with 'usual degree' weakens the protection obligations. This creates ambiguity and potential conflict with the 'highest degree of care' to protect the Confidential information, creating a In-Text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Non-disclosure and Restriction on Use The Receiving Party agrees to retain the Material in confidence a nd not to use the Material for any purpose other than in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and of the Consortium Agreement. In Particular, the Receiving Party undertakes (in addition and wit hout prejudice to any commitments under the Consortium Agreement), that: a. the Receiving Party shall not disclose the Material to any Third Party except with the Steering Committee’s prior written consent an d provided that no othe r legal disclosure requirement applies; b. the Material shall neither be copied, nor otherwise reproduced nor duplicated in whole or in part where such copying, reproduction or duplication have not been specifically authorized by the Consortium Agreement or otherwise approved in writing by the Steering Committee; The Receiving Party acknowledges that the covena nts of non-disclosure and non-use in this Agreement shall be effective in every country in the world. The Receiving Party shall take commercially reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or improper use of the Material, to prevent the material from falling into the public domain, and to protect it from falling into the possession of Third Parties. Such measures include, but shall not be limited to, the usual degree of care that the Receiving Party utilises to protect its own confidential information. In the ev ent of loss or theft of any documents, items of work in progress, or any work products embodying Material, the Consortium Secretariat must be notified immediately by the Receiving Party, who shall cooperate fully in remedying same.
perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation explicitly states that the change weakens the protection obligations by changing 'all reasonable measures' to 'commercially reasonable measures' and 'highest degree' to 'usual degree'. The contradicted text includes the modified text. Therefore, the changed text replaces stronger protective language with weaker language, directly conflicting with an implicit expectation of a higher standard of protection. This causes an In-Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In the ev ent of loss or theft of any documents, items of work in progress, or any work products embodying Material, the Consortium Secretariat must be notified immediately by the Receiving Party, who shall cooperate fully in remedying same.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event of loss or theft of *tangible* documents, items of work in progress, or any work products embodying Material, the Consortium Secretariat must be notified *within 30 days* by the Receiving Party, who shall cooperate *as reasonably requested* in remedying same.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states "immediately" now it states "within 30 days" which creates ambiguity. The original text states "cooperate fully" now it states "cooperate *as reasonably requested* in remedying same.". This limits the Receiving Party's duty to cooperate and the timeframe to notify the secretarait, contradicting the requirement to prevent material from falling into the public domain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Non-disclosure and Restriction on Use The Receiving Party agrees to retain the Material in confidence a nd not to use the Material for any purpose other than in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and of the Consortium Agreement. In Particular, the Receiving Party undertakes (in addition and wit hout prejudice to any commitments under the Consortium Agreement), that: a. the Receiving Party shall not disclose the Material to any Third Party except with the Steering Committee’s prior written consent an d provided that no othe r legal disclosure requirement applies; b. the Material shall neither be copied, nor otherwise reproduced nor duplicated in whole or in part where such copying, reproduction or duplication have not been specifically authorized by the Consortium Agreement or otherwise approved in writing by the Steering Committee; The Receiving Party acknowledges that the covena nts of non-disclosure and non-use in this Agreement shall be effective in every country in the world. The Receiving Party shall take commercially reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or improper use of the Material, to prevent the material from falling into the public domain, and to protect it from falling into the possession of Third Parties. Such measures include, but shall not be limited to, the usual degree of care that the Receiving Party utilises to protect its own confidential information. In the ev ent of loss or theft of any tangible documents, items of work in progress, or any work products embodying Material, the Consortium Secretariat must be notified within 30 days by the Receiving Party, who shall cooperate as reasonably requested in remedying same.
perturbation.justification: The original text required immediate notification of loss or theft and full cooperation. The changed text modifies this to 'within 30 days' and 'as reasonably requested', directly contradicting the requirement in the Contradicted Text to 'prevent the material from falling into the public domain' and to protect the secrecy of the material, as a delay of 30 days and only reasonable cooperation might not be sufficient to prevent this.

perturbation.original_text: Should the Receiving Party violate any of the terms and conditions of this Agreement, the Consortium Members shall be entitled, in addition to any other remedies that maybe available, in law, in equity or otherwise, to obtain injunctive relief against the threatened br each of this Agreement or the continuation of any such breach, without the nece ssity of proving actual damages.
perturbation.changed_text: Should the Receiving Party *willfully* violate any of the terms and conditions of this Agreement, the Consortium Members *may*, in addition to any other remedies that maybe available, in law, in equity or otherwise, *consider* obtaining injunctive relief against the threatened br each of this Agreement or the continuation of any such breach, *provided they can demonstrate significant financial harm*.
perturbation.explanation: The changed term shifts “any of the terms and conditions” to “willfully” making violations only eligible if “willful”. The changed term shifts “shall” to “may”. The change also means there is a requirement to show “significant financial harm” before the option to consider obtaining injunctive relief. By changing the requirements to get legal relieve against an agreement, contradicts the text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Indemnity The Receiving Party acknowledges and agrees that any breach of this Agreement would cause immediate and irreparable injury to the Consorti um and its Members. Should the Receiving Party willfully violate any of the terms and conditions of this Agreement, the Consortium Members may be entitled, in addition to any other remedies that maybe available, in law, in equity or otherwise, to consider obtaining injunctive relief against the threatened br each of this Agreement or the continuation of any such breach, provided they can demonstrate significant financial harm. In addition, in case of violation of this Agreemen t, the Receiving Party may be expelled from the Consortium in accordance with the conditions provided in Section 4 of the Consortium Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by altering the conditions under which the Consortium Members can seek injunctive relief. Originally, any violation of the agreement allowed them to obtain injunctive relief without proving actual damages. The changed text limits this to 'willful' violations, requires demonstration of 'significant financial harm', and changes 'shall' to 'may', effectively giving the Consortium Members the option to 'consider' obtaining injunctive relief instead of guaranteeing it. This directly contradicts the contradicted text by adding additional requirements and reducing the certainty of obtaining injunctive relief for a breach of the agreement. Additionally, the contradicted text mentions that the Receiving Party may be expelled from the Consortium in accordance with the conditions provided in Section 4, which does not align with the changed conditions for seeking injunctive relief, thus creating a misalignment within the agreement.
